study: SG-WF-004 taddhita residue -in/-ika/-ya — hand-adjudication of the seeded base-attested sample

categories.taddhita: genuine secondary derivation from a nominal base (relational/possessive/patronymic)
categories.krt: primary/deverbal — agentive -in or gerundive -ya on a verbal root (vibhraṃśin, prāpya)
categories.compound: the suffix sits on a compound final member (supathin, giriśaya)

ika.note: relational vṛддхi -ika is a clean taddhita marker — no kṛt homonym

in.note: dominated by kṛt AGENTIVE -in (vibhraṃśin, pramathin) — same ending, same mfn POS, same base-attestation as possessive taddhita

ya.note: dominated by kṛt GERUNDIVE -ya (prāpya, sambhāṣya) — the polyfunctional ending Whitney flags

verdict: MW POS + base-attestation cleanly recovers -ika (relational, ~100%), but NOT -in or -ya: possessive/patronymic taddhita is homonymous with kṛt agentive/gerundive on the same ending, POS (mfn) and attested base — a genuinely hard border no lexical filter here separates. The POS-grounded token counts are therefore precision-adjusted below.
